notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: NodePool lifecycle exposes CREATING, ACTIVE, UPDATING, DELETING, DELETED, FAILED, INACTIVE, and NEEDS_ATTENTION. Repo-authored controller success is ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or NEEDS_ATTENTION, requeue covers CREATING and UPDATING, FAILED is terminal without requeue, and delete confirmation follows DELETING to DELETED.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: UpdateNodePoolDetails exposes name, kubernetes_version, initial_node_labels, quantity_per_subnet, subnet_ids, node_config_details, node_metadata, node_source_details, ssh_public_key, node_shape, node_shape_config, freeform_tags, defined_tags, node_eviction_node_pool_settings, and node_pool_cycling_details as in-place update fields. Fields absent from UpdateNodePoolDetails remain create-only drift and must not open implicit replacement.

CreateNodePool, UpdateNodePool, and DeleteNodePool each return opc-work-request-id and opc-request-id headers. The refreshed generatedruntime client records those header breadcrumbs in shared status while lifecycle rereads, not helper-owned work-request waits, drive async.current and steady-state readiness.

ListNodePoolsRequest filters by compartmentId, clusterId, lifecycleState, and name and returns Items []NodePoolSummary. Safe reusable matching is exact compartment_id plus cluster_id plus name, restricted to reusable ACTIVE, CREATING, UPDATING, INACTIVE, or NEEDS_ATTENTION candidates; duplicate exact-name matches must fail instead of guessing.
